title: EDHOC: C_I/C_R negative integer encoding inconsistent with RFC 9528
description:
PROTOCOL: Connection identifier decoding for negative integers may not match RFC 9528 encoding rules.

Locations:
- Lines 509-516 (initiator decoding C_R)
- Lines 869-876 (responder decoding C_I)

Code:
} else if (c_r_int >= -24 && c_r_int < 0) {
    ctx->c_r[0] = (uint8_t)(c_r_int + 256);
    ctx->c_r_len = 1;
}

Per RFC 9528 Section 3.3.2, connection identifiers use the 'bstr_identifier' type:
- One-byte integers -24 to 23 encode as CBOR integers
- Longer values encode as byte strings

The decoding stores negative integers as (c_r_int + 256), e.g., -1 becomes 255.

Issues:
1. Later comparison/use of c_r[0] will see 255, not -1
2. When encoding C_R in message_2 (lines 994-1004), it checks ctx->c_r[0] <= 23 - this won't match for values that were originally negative
3. Round-trip may not preserve value

Impact: Connection identifiers with negative CBOR values may not round-trip correctly.

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:509-516,869-876,994-1004
notes:
Commit: 28999d0f3
Decoding stores negative C_I/C_R as 232-255 in uint8 buffer; encoding reverses to negative CBOR int (zcbor_int32_put with -24..-1). Matches RFC 9528 bstr_identifier. Roundtrip works. P2 fixed.
close_reason: Negative CID handling consistent with RFC (232-255 internal rep); commit 28999d0f3
